_meta.description: Festival rules using LUNAR MONTH NAMES (correct Adhik Maas model)
_meta.notes: Purnimant festival masa (default month_model=festival) with Adhik lag; MoHA overrides for public holidays. v3.1: full solar/lunar catalog — solar festivals carry bs_month/solar_day (sankranti calendar), ad_fixed carry ad_month/ad_day, lunar carry lunar_month/tithi/paksha.

teej.notes: Dar Khane Din to Rishi Panchami

janai-purnima.notes: Sacred thread ceremony; Purnimant Shrawan Purnima with Adhik/BS civil lag

vijaya-dashami.notes: Main day of Dashain — Devi Visarjan

bs-new-year.notes: Baishakh 1 — official BS calendar first day

ganatantra-diwas.notes: Jestha 15 — Nepal became a republic on this day in BS 2065

shahid-diwas.notes: Magh 16 — martyrs of the democracy movement

loktantra-diwas.notes: Baishakh 11 — people's movement II of BS 2062/63

prithvi-jayanti.notes: Poush 27 — birth anniversary of Prithvi Narayan Shah

ram-navami.notes: Chaitra Shukla 9 — birth of Lord Ram

tamu-lhosar.notes: Gurung New Year — Poush 15 BS; approximately December 30 every year

sonam-lhosar.notes: Tamang New Year — Magh Shukla Pratipada; overrides used for exact MoHA dates

gyalpo-lhosar.notes: Tibetan/Sherpa New Year — approximately Falgun Shukla 2; overrides used for exact MoHA dates

mata-tirtha-aunshi.notes: Mother's Day Nepal — Baishakh Amavasya (Krishna Paksha 15)

sithi-nakha.notes: Newari festival — Jestha Shukla Shashthi; Kumar Shashti, god of underground springs

haribodhi-ekadashi.notes: Kartik Shukla 11 — Vishnu wakes from Chaturmas sleep; Devotthanam Ekadashi

harishayani-ekadashi.notes: Ashadh Shukla 11 — Vishnu begins Chaturmas; Tulsi planting

magh-purnima.notes: Magh full moon — end of Swasthani Brata; sacred river bath day

vivah-panchami.notes: Mangsir Shukla 5 — celestial marriage of Ram and Sita at Janakpur

navaratri-chaitra.notes: Chaitra Shukla 1–9 spring Navaratri; Durga Ashtami on day 8, Ram Navami on day 9

terai-holi.notes: Terai Holi — day after Falgun Purnima (Chaitra Shukla 1)

maghi-parwa.notes: Tharu/Magar/Chhantyaala New Year — Magh 1, coincides with Makara Sankranti

gaura-parwa.notes: Far-western Gauri worship — Bhadra Krishna Ashtami (Durvashtami)

kartik-purnima.notes: Tulsi vivah closes; Kartik snan ends

bala-chaturdashi.notes: Satbij scattering at Pashupati for the departed

swasthani-brata-arambha.notes: Month-long Swasthani katha begins — Poush Purnima

bhanu-jayanti.notes: Birth of Adikavi Bhanubhakta Acharya — Asar 29

prajatantra-diwas.notes: Falgun 7 — end of Rana rule (BS 2007); MoHA public holiday

mesh-sankranti.notes: Sun enters Aries — Baishakh 1 solar calendar

gorakhkali-puja.notes: Also observed monthly on Shukla Ashtami

chandi-purnima.notes: Same tithi as Buddha Jayanti / Ubhauli

caste-untouchability-abolition-day.name_en: National Day for Abolition of Caste Discrimination and Untouchability
caste-untouchability-abolition-day.name_ne: जातीय भेदभाव तथा छुवाछुत उन्मुलन राष्ट्रिय दिवस

chaturmas-vrata-arambha.notes: Four-month monsoon vrata — with Hari Shayani Ekadashi

vyas-jayanti.notes: Birth of Ved Vyasa — Ashadh Purnima

world-day-against-human-trafficking.name_en: World Day Against Trafficking in Persons

world-indigenous-peoples-day.name_en: International Day of the World's Indigenous Peoples

world-day-against-enforced-disappearance.name_en: International Day of the Victims of Enforced Disappearances

world-disaster-reduction-day.name_en: International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction

world-poverty-eradication-day.name_en: International Day for the Eradication of Poverty

world-science-for-peace-day.name_en: World Science Day for Peace and Development
world-science-for-peace-day.name_ne: शान्ति र विकासको लागि विश्व विज्ञान दिवस

world-heritage-trafficking-day.name_en: International Day Against Illicit Trafficking of Cultural Property
world-heritage-trafficking-day.name_ne: सांस्कृतिक सम्पत्तिको अवैध ओसारपसार विरुद्धको अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय दिवस

world-nuclear-disarmament-day.name_en: International Day for the Total Elimination of Nuclear Weapons
